evidence_quotes.quote: protecting already purchased video games under the Digital Fairness Act.

paragraphs.text: Hi, I would just like to add a suggestion to include protecting already purchased video games under the Digital Fairness Act. Companies have a lot of control over digital items, such as video games, that have been sold and bought by consumers, to the extent that any point, said company can revoke those games purchased by the consumer at ANY time, without refunding them or giving them any say in it. It's thoroughly invasive, and unfair to take away something that someone paid hard-earned money for. It's important to protect these games not just for that reason, but because video games are a form of art just like books and movies are, and deserve to be preserved for future generations so everyone can enjoy them. Please consider putting regulations for preserving video games in the Digital Fairness Act.
